title: AI foundations capstone deterministic AI draft bank

publication.changeSummary: Misconception MCQ, evidence-boundary fill-gap for AI foundations capstone; all items remain blocked from formal exams pending independent human review.

items.title: AI foundations capstone: reject the overclaim
items.prompt: Which statement is source-bounded and corrects this AI misconception? A polished AI demo with a strong average score is a complete capstone and can be described as production-ready.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted unreviewed draft; independent human technical, source, pedagogy, safety, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Choose the statement that names mechanism, evidence conditions, and the boundary of the claim rather than a fluent or benchmark-based overgeneralization.

feedback.correct: Correct. Deliver bounded implementation plus baseline, reproducibility, provenance, evaluation slices, stress failures, oversight, risk controls, and claims limited to the recorded evidence.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the overclaim. Deliver bounded implementation plus baseline, reproducibility, provenance, evaluation slices, stress failures, oversight, risk controls, and claims limited to the recorded evidence.

criteria.description: Selects the source-bounded correction and rejects the documented AI overclaim.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against cited sources and ensure the distractor does not normalize unsafe or overstated claims.

options.text: Deliver bounded implementation plus baseline, reproducibility, provenance, evaluation slices, stress failures, oversight, risk controls, and claims limited to the recorded evidence.
A polished AI demo with a strong average score is a complete capstone and can be described as production-ready.

answer.explanation: Deliver bounded implementation plus baseline, reproducibility, provenance, evaluation slices, stress failures, oversight, risk controls, and claims limited to the recorded evidence.

items.title: AI foundations capstone: canonical evidence boundary
items.prompt: Complete the exact source-bounded correction phrase; the complete sentence must remain an honest beginner-to-intermediate claim.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted unreviewed draft; independent human technical, source, pedagogy, safety, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Retrieve the short checkpoint from the correction and ensure it narrows rather than expands the evidence claim.

feedback.correct: Correct. The checkpoint is 'claims limited'. Deliver bounded implementation plus baseline, reproducibility, provenance, evaluation slices, stress failures, oversight, risk controls, and claims limited to the recorded evidence.
feedback.incorrect: The phrase must preserve the technical and responsible-AI boundary. Deliver bounded implementation plus baseline, reproducibility, provenance, evaluation slices, stress failures, oversight, risk controls, and claims limited to the recorded evidence.

criteria.description: Supplies the exact canonical phrase that completes the bounded source-aligned correction.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the accepted phrase and technically equivalent variants before learner use.
items.template: Deliver bounded implementation plus baseline, reproducibility, provenance, evaluation slices, stress failures, oversight, risk controls, and [[checkpoint]] to the recorded evidence.

answer.explanation: Deliver bounded implementation plus baseline, reproducibility, provenance, evaluation slices, stress failures, oversight, risk controls, and claims limited to the recorded evidence.
